behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is local parsing logic (string-to-segments) with no required module handoff, I/O, subprocess, or external dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function directly with a multiline block like `A=1\nB=2\ngh pr create --title x --body y` and assert it returns ordered discrete segments where `gh pr create ...` is its own segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: The incident came from parser output being consumed by trigger classification. A unit test of either piece alone can miss the real handoff bug.
behaviors.test_description: Run PostToolUse trigger detection through real parser+classifier wiring on a multiline command block ending in `gh pr create ...`; assert classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the same cross-module boundary risk as behavior 2, but for false positives across command variants; parser/classifier interaction must be exercised together.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the wired parser+classifier path on a multiline block ending with `gh issue create ...`; assert result is non-PR trigger and no PR review-loop action is emitted.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often occur in subprocess output/URL reconstruction plus real filesystem persistence. Mocks can hide formatting and persistence edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook in a real test process with actual command-result payload shape (including multiline shell context) and verify a real state file is written containing a valid reconstructed PR identifier that matches expected repo/number format.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This boundary spans separate hook executions/processes communicating through persisted state. In-process mocks can pass while real cross-process/file contract fails.
behaviors.test_description: Execute PostToolUse to write state, then invoke PreToolUse in a separate process/context against that state file; assert PreToolUse reads it successfully and enforces the review gate decision.
